LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS COMPLAINANT SALESFORCE.COM INC.'S (D.B.A. DELAWARE SALESFORCE.COM) MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF FIRST AND EIGHTH CAUSES TO ACTION AND PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IN FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF AND CROSS-DEFENDANT MEDICAL STAFFING NETWORK, INC. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: MOTION TO STRIKE AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION IS GRANTED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. MOTION TO STRIKE AS TO THE EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE BREACH OF CIRCUMSTANCES WITHIN THE CONTROL OF DEFENDANTS. PUNITIVE DAMAGES GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD FACTS DEMONSTRATING THAT AN OFFICER, DIRECTOR OR MANAGING AGENT COMMITTED OR RATIFIED THE ACTS. COUNSEL TO AGREE UPON AN ORDER AND SUBMIT TO THE COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR # 8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-COMPLAINANT SALESFORCE.COM INC.'S (D.B.A. DELAWARE SALESFORCE.COM) DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. DEMURRER AS TO THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF PLEADS FACTS TO MEET THE ELEMENTS OF NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION BUT FAILS TO ALLEGE THAT THE FRAUD CONSISTS OF ANYTHING MORE THAN A FUTURE PROMISE. UNDER TARMAN V STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTO INSURANCE (1991) 2CALAPP4TH 153, 158, THERE IS NO TORT FOR A NEGLIGENT FALSE PROMISE. DEMURRER AS TO THE 6TH AND 7TH CAUSES OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED RESTORATION OF THE PROPERTY. CIVIL CODE SECTION 1691(B). DEMURRER AS TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. DEMURRER AS TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE DEFENDANT'S BREACH WAS AS A CONSEQUENCE OF CIRCUMSTANCES WITHIN ITS CONTROL. DEMURRER AS TO THE 8TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED; THE UNDERLYING CAUSES OF ACTION HAVE NOT BEEN SHOWN TO FAIL. DEMURRER AS TO THE 9TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED, TARMAN DOES NOT REQUIRE WHERE THE ALLEGATIONS WERE MADE. DEMURRER AS TO THE 10TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO DESCRIBE IN GREATER DETAIL THE SCHEME TO MISLEAD & DETAILS RELATED TO THE DECEPTIVE NATURE OF ADVERTISING AND PROMOTIONAL MATERIAL. DEMURRER AS TO THE 11TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. CALIFORNIA ALLOWS EXTRINSIC EVIDENCE TO ESTABLISH A DIFFERENT MEANING TO UNAMBIGUOUS LANGUAGE. P. G. & E. VS. G. W. THOMAS DRAYAGE. COUNSEL TO AGREE UPON AN ORDER AND SUBMIT TO THE COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR# 8600

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of First And Eighth Causes To Action Fand Prayer For Punitive Damages In First Amended Complaint Filed By Pltf And Cross-Deft Medical Staffing Network,Inc.
Off Calendar Apr-18-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS COMPLAINANT SALESFORCE.COM INC.'S (D.B.A. DELAWARE SALESFORCE.COM) MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF FIRST AND EIGHTH CAUSES TO ACTION AND PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IN FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF AND CROSS-DEFENDANT MEDICAL STAFFING NETWORK, INC.
APPEARANCES: DAVID P. DAPPER, ESQUIRE (AKERMAN SENTERFITT, 213.688.9500) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. DOUG WINTHROP, ESQUIRE (HOWARD RICE NEMEROVSKI CANADY FALK & RABIN, 415.677.3498) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: MOTION TO STRIKE AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION IS GRANTED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. MOTION TO STRIKE AS TO THE EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE BREACH OF CIRCUMSTANCES WITHIN THE CONTROL OF DEFENDANTS. PUNITIVE DAMAGES GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD FACTS DEMONSTRATING THAT AN OFFICER, DIRECTOR OR MANAGING AGENT COMMITTED OR RATIFIED THE ACTS. COUNSEL TO AGREE UPON AN ORDER AND SUBMIT TO THE COURT. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR # 8600

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-18-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Cross-complainant Salesforce.Com Inc.'s (d.b.a. Delaware Salesforce.Com) Demurrer to First Amended Complaint.
Appearances: David P. Dapper, Esquire (Akerman Senterfitt, 213.688.9500) appearing for plaintiff. Doug Winthrop, Esquire (Howard Rice 415.677.3498) appearing for defendant.
Ruling: Argued and the Court adopts and modifies its tentative ruling: sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer as to the 4th Cause of Action is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. Demurrer as to the 5th Cause of Action is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff pleads facts to meet the elements of negligent misrepresentation but fails to allege that the fraud consists of anything more than a future promise. Under Tarman v State Farm Mutual Auto Insurance (1991) 2CalApp4th 153, 158, there is no tort for a negligent false promise. Demurrer as to the 6th and 7th Causes of Action is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. Plaintiff has not alleged restoration of the property. Civil Code Section 1691(b). Demurrer as to the 2nd Cause of Action is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. Demurrer as to the 3rd Cause of Action is sustained with leave to amend to allege defendant's breach was as a consequence of circumstances within its control. Demurrer as to the 8th Cause of Action is overruled; the underlying causes of action have not been shown to fail. Demurrer as to the 9th Cause of Action is overruled, Tarman does not require where the allegations were made. Demurrer as to the 10th Cause of Action is sustained with leave to amend to describe in greater detail the scheme to mislead & details related to the deceptive nature of advertising and promotional material. Demurrer as to the 11th Cause of Action is overruled. California allows extrinsic evidence to establish a different meaning to unambiguous language. P. G. & E. vs. G. W. Thomas Drayage. Counsel to agree upon an order and submit to the court. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPOR
